title: Callista rugosa
intro: Callista rugosa, also known as the orchid tree or wild orchid, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating conditions such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Callista rugosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Callista rugosa is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the form of tinctures, capsules, or powders to leverage its health benefits.
taxonomy: Callista rugosa is classified within the family Cardiidae, which includes various types of marine clams. This classification is based on the shell characteristics and the animal's behavior. The genus Callista is part of the family Cardiidae, with several species identified globally. The classification of Callista rugosa is further grouped into the subfamily Cardiinae. This classification helps in the understanding and identification of the species.
